# Tracking Requirements Checklist: Tracking Phase 0

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, and completeness of Phase 0 tracking requirements before planning
**Created**: 2026-05-26
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are all Phase 0 capture paths required by the development plan represented in the spec, including in-home voice, phone voice, Signal text, and optional Signal photo? [Completeness, Spec §User Stories 1/3/4, Spec §FR-010..FR-013]
- [ ] CHK002 Are normal track and checkout/displaced intents specified for every required capture path? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-010..FR-012]
- [ ] CHK003 Are tracking record attributes complete enough to support append-only storage, retrieval, source provenance, displaced state, optional photo reference, and optional supersession? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §Key Entities]
- [ ] CHK004 Are query/open activity requirements documented enough to support later loop-closure planning without surfacing prompts in Phase 0? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009, Spec §Tracking Query]
- [ ] CHK005 Are explicit out-of-scope boundaries documented for prompts, duplicate prompts, board view, semantic retrieval, polished surface views, and local speech-to-text migration? [Completeness, Spec §Assumptions]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is the meaning of displaced state clear and limited to the newest known checked-out/away-from-expected-place model? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002, Spec §Displaced State]
- [ ] CHK007 Is the spec clear that free-form text is the canonical tracking content and that item/location parsing is not required during capture? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-016]
- [ ] CHK008 Is the phrase "same authorization posture" specific enough for planning to map it to existing agent and surface auth without ambiguity? [Clarity, Spec §FR-015]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "newest relevant match" defined clearly enough to drive acceptance criteria for search result ordering? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Spec §User Story 2]
- [ ] CHK010 Is the fallback behavior for no exact search matches specified clearly enough to avoid a dead-end retrieval experience? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 2]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK011 Are append-only requirements consistent across duplicate text, stale records, supersession, and correction-by-new-record behavior? [Consistency, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-017]
- [ ] CHK012 Are timestamp requirements consistent between capture time, receive time, and the assumption that users do not manually choose timestamps? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK013 Are Phase 0 retrieval requirements consistent with the stated boundary that this phase is substrate-only and not a full workflow? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK014 Are Signal text and Signal photo requirements consistent about searchable caption text and optional image reference preservation? [Consistency, Spec §FR-012..FR-013, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK015 Are accessibility requirements scoped consistently to user-facing search/open surfaces and setup feedback rather than backend-only substrate work? [Consistency, Spec §Accessibility & Language Governance]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK016 Are all success criteria measurable without depending on a specific implementation technology? [Measurability, Spec §Success Criteria]
- [ ] CHK017 Is the 5-second and 10-second searchability target tied to the correct capture paths and normal connectivity assumption? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK018 Is the smoke-test completion criterion specific enough to distinguish successful substrate validation from full workflow completion? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK019 Are success criteria sufficient to validate that tracking is useful from the first record without inventory completion or cleanup? [Measurability, Spec §SC-008]
- [ ] CHK020 Are acceptance scenarios independently testable for each prioritized user story? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK021 Are primary write-and-search flows covered for both normal and displaced records? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 2]
- [ ] CHK022 Are portable/out-of-home capture scenarios covered separately from in-room voice capture? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §User Story 4]
- [ ] CHK023 Are no-match and near-match retrieval scenarios covered as requirements rather than deferred entirely to implementation choice? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2]
- [ ] CHK024 Are setup phrase collision scenarios covered for both in-home and phone voice capture paths? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK025 Are Phase 0 coverage gaps for rooms without a voice device documented as expected rather than treated as failures? [Coverage, Spec §Assumptions]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK026 Are invalid or missing tracking text, source, displaced state, capture time, and supersession inputs all addressed in requirement-level edge cases? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK027 Are duplicate item wording and stale/wrong records addressed in a way that preserves the model-not-ledger principle? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §Tracking Record]
- [ ] CHK028 Are friction observations covered as ordinary free-form tracks without introducing a separate feedback workflow? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-020]
- [ ] CHK029 Is assistant interception defined as a Phase 0 verification failure condition clearly enough for acceptance planning? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §SC-006]
- [ ] CHK030 Are photo-caption and missing-image edge cases specified enough to preserve searchability when photo tracking is used? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-013]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK031 Are security and privacy requirements specified for both tracking writes and user tracking data retrieval? [Security, Spec §FR-015]
- [ ] CHK032 Are performance expectations limited to user-visible Phase 0 validation targets rather than premature system-scale claims? [Performance, Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK033 Are accessibility requirements explicit enough for any persistent search/open surface introduced during Phase 0? [Accessibility, Spec §Accessibility & Language Governance]
- [ ] CHK034 Is bilingual delivery explicitly marked N/A with a rationale tied to current product copy and milestone scope? [Localization, Spec §Accessibility & Language Governance]
- [ ] CHK035 Are backup expectations scoped clearly enough to distinguish main tracking data from future photo file backup needs? [Reliability, Spec §Assumptions]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK036 Are dependencies on existing Lattice authentication, capture routing, Signal handling, and backups documented at the requirement level? [Dependency, Spec §FR-014..FR-015, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK037 Are external device assumptions for the printing-room voice device, phone assistant routing, TV hotword behavior, and unplugged home speakers represented clearly enough for planning? [Assumption, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §User Story 4, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK038 Is Signal voice transcription explicitly excluded from Phase 0 acceptance while Signal text remains required? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK039 Are future-phase dependencies such as loop-closure prompts, duplicate prompts, board view, and semantic retrieval deferred without weakening Phase 0 acceptance? [Dependency, Spec §Assumptions]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK040 Is the optional supersession behavior clear enough to avoid accidental auto-supersession in Phase 0 planning? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-001, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK041 Is the relationship between "closest available matches" and a clear "no stored tracks yet" response unambiguous enough for no-result retrieval requirements? [Ambiguity, Spec §User Story 2]
- [ ] CHK042 Is the source provenance requirement specific enough to distinguish capture paths while avoiding user-entered categories or zones? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-016, Spec §Capture Path]
- [ ] CHK043 Are any requirements using SHOULD, especially Signal photo support, intentionally optional and reflected consistently in success criteria? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-013, Spec §SC-001]
- [ ] CHK044 Are there any conflicts between accepting missing capture time consistently and requiring capture time as a core tracking attribute? [Conflict, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-005]

## Notes

- This checklist validates the requirements text, not the implementation.
- Items should be checked off when the spec is clear, complete, consistent, and measurable enough for planning.
